vars.conversation: @kody replying to your review on this test: here we always keep dependency injection, not global mocks, even if it's shorter.
vars.expectedRule: always use dependency injection instead of global mocks in tests

vars.conversation: @kody on this DB config suggestion, please remember our standard: PostgreSQL is our primary database, not MySQL.
vars.expectedRule: primary database is PostgreSQL, not MySQL

vars.conversation: @kody on your SQL suggestion in this file, please remember we prefer EXISTS over COUNT(*) for existence checks.
vars.expectedRule: prefer EXISTS over COUNT(*) for SQL existence checks

vars.conversation: @kody about your review in this domain service: we are strict hexagonal here, domain must not depend on infrastructure classes.
vars.expectedRule: domain layer must have zero dependencies on infrastructure layer

vars.conversation: @kody on this env-variable review comment, memorize this rule: frontend-exposed env vars must start with NEXT_PUBLIC_.
vars.expectedRule: frontend-exposed environment variables must start with NEXT_PUBLIC_

vars.conversation: @kody replying to your refactor suggestion in this util: we avoid lodash here, please stick to native array methods.
vars.expectedRule: prefer native JavaScript array methods over Lodash

vars.conversation: @kody for this date formatting suggestion, remember our project standard is date-fns, not moment.js.
vars.expectedRule: use date-fns instead of moment.js for date handling

vars.conversation: @kody on this module export review, keep this as a rule for next suggestions: we don't use default exports in TypeScript files.
vars.expectedRule: use named exports only in TypeScript files

vars.conversation: @kody from this route review, save this convention: all API routes should be prefixed with /api/v1.
vars.expectedRule: API routes must be prefixed with /api/v1

vars.conversation: @kody replying to your auth suggestion here: remember that we authenticate with JWT tokens in request headers.
vars.expectedRule: authentication is done via JWT tokens in headers
